The Challenge

Manual Tool Management
is Broken

Industrial workshops lose thousands of hours annually to inefficient tool tracking, misplaced equipment, and manual sorting processes that stall productivity.

30%
Productivity LostWorkers spend nearly a third of their time searching for misplaced tools instead of executing core tasks.
$8B
Annual LossesThe manufacturing sector loses billions each year to poor tool management and inventory tracking failures.
4×
Slower SortingManual classification and sorting is four times slower than an automated vision-based system.
0
Real-Time TrackingTraditional workshops have zero visibility into real-time tool inventory — no logs, no accountability.
🔧
Tools Get Misplaced
Without a structured system, tools are returned to wrong locations constantly, creating workflow bottlenecks.
⏱️
Time-Consuming Sorting
Manual sorting requires trained personnel and is prone to errors, especially under high-volume conditions.
📋
No Smart Tracking
Legacy systems rely on clipboards and manual logs — no real-time data, no accountability, no optimization.
The System

Intelligent Automation,
End to End

Tool Tally integrates computer vision, machine learning, and precision mechanics into one cohesive system — making tool management effortless and error-free.

01
👁️
Computer Vision
High-resolution camera captures tool images in real time. OpenCV preprocessing pipeline cleans and normalizes input for ML inference.
02
🧠
ML Classification
Trained neural network identifies tool type with 96%+ accuracy across pliers, screwdrivers, and wrenches.
03
🍓
Raspberry Pi Control
Raspberry Pi 4B acts as the brain — processing model output, managing GPIO signals, and orchestrating all mechanical components.
04
⚙️
Servo & Flap Actuation
Precision servo motors drive classification flaps. Each flap routes a tool to the correct bin with repeatability under 2mm.
05
📲
User Interface
Clean touchscreen UI for Take and Deposit workflows. Locks, unlocks, and guides users through every interaction seamlessly.
06
🚀
Linear Actuation
Linear actuator precisely pushes classified tools onto the ramp system, ensuring controlled delivery to bins without jamming.
System Flow

The Complete
Workflow

Every interaction follows a precisely engineered sequence — from user intent to sorted tool, with zero manual decision-making required.

STEP 01
User Interaction
User selects Take Tool or Deposit Tool via the touch interface. Clear UI guides each session from start to finish.
UI / UX Layer
1
2
STEP 02
Image Capture
The overhead camera captures a high-resolution image automatically using a proximity trigger on tool placement.
OpenCV · Camera Module
STEP 03
ML Classification
The captured image is passed through the trained CNN. The model outputs a confidence-weighted class label: Plier, Screwdriver, or Wrench.
CNN Model · TFLite
3
4
STEP 04
Raspberry Pi Processing
The Raspberry Pi 4B receives the classification result and determines the target bin. It sends GPIO signals to activate the appropriate flap.
GPIO · Embedded Control
STEP 05
Flap & Actuator
The corresponding sorting flap opens via servo. The linear actuator pushes the tool onto the ramp, guiding it into the correct bin.
Servo Motor · Linear Actuator
5
6
STEP 06
System Reset
Once delivery is confirmed, all actuators and flaps reset. The system returns to standby in under 5 seconds, ready for the next cycle.
State Machine · Auto-Reset
Capabilities

Built with
Precision

Every technical module is engineered for reliability, speed, and seamless integration within the complete system architecture.

👁️
Computer Vision
Real-time image acquisition. OpenCV pipeline handles noise reduction, edge detection, and normalization before inference.
OpenCV
🧠
ML Classification
Custom-trained CNN achieves 96%+ accuracy across 3 tool categories with robust generalization to varying orientations.
TensorFlow Lite
🍓
Raspberry Pi 4B
Manages all GPIO outputs, processes ML inference, controls display UI, and coordinates the full electromechanical sequence.
GPIO · Linux
⚙️
Servo Actuation
PWM-controlled servo motors drive 3 independent sorting flaps with ±1° positional accuracy.
PWM · Servo
➡️
Linear Actuator
Delivers consistent push force to guide tools from the classification chamber to the ramp.
Mechanical
📲
Touchscreen UI
Full-featured GUI for Take/Deposit workflows with real-time feedback and bin status overview.
Tkinter · Python
🔒
Lock System
Tool-specific locks engage or release based on user selection — ensuring only the requested tool type is accessible.
Solenoid Lock
♻️
Auto-Reset Logic
After each cycle, the system autonomously resets all actuators and UI state — sub-5-second turnaround.
State Machine
Hardware

ComputeRaspberry Pi 4B · 4GB
CameraPi Camera v2 · 8MP
Servos3× SG90 · PWM Control
Actuator12V DC Linear Actuator
Display7" Touchscreen DSI
ML ModelCustom CNN · TFLite
ClassificationPlier · Screwdriver · Wrench
Accuracy96.2% Top-1
Cycle Time< 8 seconds end-to-end

Validated
Results

Rigorous evaluation with held-out test sets confirms strong generalization. The classifier exceeds production-grade thresholds across all three tool classes.

The Future

Smart Workshops
Start Here

Tool Tally demonstrates that automated tool management is not just possible — it is practical, scalable, and ready for deployment. From engineering labs to industrial floors, intelligent sorting systems like this represent the next evolution in workspace efficiency.
